content:
SUBJECT: AI Gym Day 47: The one-to-one operating system

The free 14 days got the reps moving. The paid track is where the habit has to become professionally useful. Today belongs to people management and delegation: one practical workout, one usable output, one more piece of your AI working system.

TODAY'S WORKOUT
---------------
STRUCTURING & ORGANISING | STEADY STATE | 20 min

THE BRIEF:
Design a recurring one-to-one that does not become calendar furniture.

THE SITUATION:
Build or improve a one-to-one structure.

PROMPT SCAFFOLD:
Do not copy this blindly. Adapt it to your work.

"I am working on [real situation]. My goal is [specific outcome]. The audience or user is [person/team/customer/reader]. Here is the source material or context I am allowed to share: [paste safe material]. The constraints are [length, tone, deadline, format, privacy, things to avoid]. Help me with: Design a recurring one-to-one that does not become calendar furniture. Before producing the final output, tell me what assumptions you are making and what information would improve the result."

YOUR REPS:
1. Ask the model for a one-to-one agenda that covers work, blockers, decisions, development, and wellbeing without becoming therapy cosplay.

2. Ask how the agenda should change for a new starter, high performer, or struggling colleague.

3. Pick the version that fits one real person.

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
Your output is good if:
- The agenda has sections with purpose.
- It adapts to the person rather than treating everyone as a meeting-shaped object.
- There is one clear follow-up mechanism.

TRANSFER:
This transfers to one-to-ones, delegation, feedback, coaching, team rituals, performance support, conflict, and the managerial burden of being clear before expecting clarity from others.

COACHING NOTE:
Recurring meetings survive because they are scheduled. They improve only when they have a designed job.

BEHAVIOURAL DESIGN:
Test the agenda once. This is your implementation intention for today: after you finish the workout, decide exactly where this output will live and when you will use it again.

MINIMUM VIABLE REP:
If today is chaos, do the smallest honest version: spend five minutes creating the input and five minutes reviewing the output. Do not aim for a masterpiece. Aim for a usable artefact and one clear improvement. Keeping the chain alive matters because skill comes from repeated contact with real work, not from admiring the concept from a safe distance.

---------------
Use only material you are allowed to share with your chosen LLM. Anonymise anything sensitive. Treat AI outputs as drafts, not authority. Verify factual claims before using them in public, financial, medical, legal, HR, or high-stakes contexts.
The AI Habit: https://harrysharman.com/projects/ai-habit/
review_notes: Generated as part of the 90-Day AI Habit paid founding track.
Continues from the free 14-day starter sequence without duplicating the foundation.
Organised by professional capability themes: creation, research, decisions, creativity, management, communication, productivity, analysis, workflow, judgement, and workplace adoption.
Draws from official prompt guidance and behavioural science principles without copying source programme content.
